CME Data-Center Issue Hits Options, Futures Trading -- 3rd Update
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao


Trading of futures and options on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange was halted for hours on Friday, disrupting some of the world's most liquid futures markets for commodities like oil, U.S. stocks and Treasuries and other financial instruments.

The exchange cited a cooling issue at a data center vendor and said it was working to resolve the problem.

The glitch kept traders of commodities such as crude oil, gold and palm oil in limbo with prices frozen, raising concerns that market volatility would intensify when the systems were brought back up.

U.S. markets were closed on Thursday for Thanksgiving, raising the importance of futures trading in Asia and Europe in providing a signal on how equities and other asset classes would move in North America when the U.S. reopens for a shortened session Friday.

Some analysts said thin trading as a result of the U.S. holiday could amplify any moves in asset prices when market action restarts.

That was becoming visible on some markets as traders in Europe began work. Treasury yields were last indicated higher by 2-3 basis points across maturities but unusually wide spreads between bid and ask levels signal patchy trade.

CME Group is one of the world's largest derivatives marketplace, operating exchanges including the New York Mercantile Exchange, Chicago Board of Trade, and the Commodity Exchange, also known as Comex.

Priyanka Sachdeva, senior market analyst at Phillip Nova, said the timing was especially unnerving, given Friday represents the last day of trading in a month, making it an important date for the settlement or rollover of contracts.

"Once trading resumes, we could see a sharp rush to unwind or roll positions, which may inject a burst of volatility and even some knee-jerk, irrational price moves as participants scramble to normalize risk," Sachdeva added.

The CME has faced disruptions before. In February 2019, trading across all markets on CME's electronic platform was halted for several hours due to technical problems. Other markets in the U.S. and Europe have also experienced occasional outages over the past decade.

One exchange particularly hamstrung in Asia was the Bursa Malaysia. Trading on Bursa Malaysia's derivatives market was halted because its products trade on CME Group's electronic trading platform, the Malaysian exchange said.

"Liquidity is already thin, so even a brief halt can distort price discovery in Treasuries, foreign exchange and commodities," said Charu Chanana, chief investment strategist at Saxo Singapore.


--Miriam Mukuru and Giulia Petroni contributed to this report


Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-28-25 0438ET

National Atomic Company Kazatomprom JSC Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
National Atomic Company Kazatomprom JSC reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was KZT 532,727 million compared to KZT 437,733 million a year ago. Net income was KZT 215,422 million compared to KZT 123,399 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was KZT 831 compared to KZT 476 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was KZT 831 compared to KZT 476 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was KZT 1,192,894 million compared to KZT 1,138,853 million a year ago. Net income was KZT 417,490 million compared to KZT 604,904 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was KZT 1,610 compared to KZT 2,332 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was KZT 1,610 compared to KZT 2,332 a year ago.

Genky Drugstores Co., Ltd. Reports Sales Results for the Month of November 2025
data.description: Genky DrugStores Co., Ltd. reported sales results for the month of November 2025. For the month, the company reported all store net sales change of 109.1% against 104.5% in 2024. Existing stores net sales change was 102.2% against 99.7% in 2024.

data.title: Tokyu Corporation Announces Dividend for the End of First Half ended September 30, 2025, Payable on December 5, 2025
data.description: Tokyu Corporation announced dividend of JPY 14.00 per share for the end of first half ended September 30, 2025 against JPY 11.00 per share paid a year ago. Scheduled date of commencement of dividend payment: December 5, 2025.

data.title: Parco Co.,Ltd. Decides Closure of Shizuoka PARCO
data.description:
J. FRONT RETAILING Co. Ltd. announced that PARCO CO., LTD., a consolidated subsidiary of the Company, has decided to close the Shizuoka PARCO as follows. Background and reason of decision on operation closures: As a commercial facility in the Shizuoka City center, Shizuoka PARCO has been operating for over 18 years, earning the warm support from the people of the local area since its opening in March 2007.

Over that time, although efforts have been made to strengthen sales capabilities and increase the efficiency of operation methods, the commercial environment surrounding the store is constantly changing, with continuous openings and renewals of competing stores both within Shizuoka City and in its vicinity in recent years. The store has been receiving support from numerous local customers. However, after carefully considering future changes in the commercial environment from a medium- to long-term perspective, and taking into account the expiration of the building lease contract, PARCO CO., LTD. has decided to close operations of the store at the end of January 2027.

Overview of Shizuoka PARCO: Address: 6-7 Kouyamachi, Aoi Ward, Shizuoka City, Shizuoka, Japan. Date of opening: March 15, 2007. Sales floor space: Approx.

31,000 m². Tenant transaction volume: ¥8,193 million (Fiscal year ended February 28, 2025). Date of termination of operation: January 31, 2027 (planned).

Future prospects: The Company plans to record a loss of approximately ¥1 billion as other operating expenses in the consolidated financial results for the first nine months of the fiscal year ending February 28, 2026, due to the store closure mentioned above. The impact of this closure has already been factored into the consolidated earnings forecasts for the fiscal year ending February 28, 2026, and there are no changes.
